name: Vonage MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Vonage MCP server providing access to Vonage's developer documentation — enabling AI agents to query API references, integration guides, and code examples for building SMS, voice, video, and messaging applications with the Vonage Communications API.
use_cases: Querying Vonage API documentation for communications development agents
Getting SMS and voice API examples for code generation agents
Accessing webhook payload documentation for integration agents
Finding Vonage SDK documentation for implementation agents
Understanding number provisioning and management APIs
not_for: Actually sending SMS or making calls (use Vonage API directly for that)
Teams using Twilio, Plivo, or other communications providers
best_when: An AI coding agent needs documentation context about Vonage APIs — especially for building SMS, voice, or video communications features.
avoid_when: You need to actually send messages — this is a documentation MCP. Use the Vonage SDK directly for production use.

auth.notes: Documentation access — no authentication required for public developer docs.

pricing.notes: Free developer documentation MCP. Vonage API itself requires account and credits.

known_agent_gotchas: Documentation-only — cannot make actual API calls to Vonage
Vonage was formerly Nexmo — older docs may reference Nexmo
SMS vs Messages API are different — Messages API is newer and preferred
Voice API and Video API have different auth (JWT vs API key)
Number Management is separate from messaging and voice APIs
agent_readiness.security_notes: No credentials required. Public documentation. HTTPS enforced. Vonage's infrastructure security.

security_score_components.security_notes: No credentials needed. Public documentation. HTTPS enforced.
